title: Tragacantha hirsuta
intro: Tragacantha hirsuta, also known as Hair-leaved Milk Vetch or Hairy Milkvetch, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its hairy leaves and clusters of pink or purple flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation and improvement of cardiovascular health, due to its ability to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha hirsuta include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacantha hirsuta include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Tragacantha hirsuta is classified as a species of plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or bean family. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and is further classified into the genus Astragalus, but is sometimes placed in the genus Tragacantha, which is often considered a synonym for Astragalus. The genus Astragalus is a large and diverse group of plants, many of which are commonly known as milkvetches. Tragacantha hirsuta is native to parts of Asia and Europe. Its classification is subject to some variation among taxonomists.
